mission.title: atlantis: Allow supplying wildcard for dir
mission.description: Allow supplying wildcard for dir. Requested by 93+ users.

steps.title: Check current atlantis deployment
steps.description:
Verify your atlantis version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
helm list -n atlantis 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working atlantis installation.

steps.title: Review atlantis configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant atlantis configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get configmap -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=atlantis
```
We define our terraform code in what we call stacks. Each stack defines multiple environments where each directory off root is the environment.

Currently we have to add a project for each directory.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow supplying wildcard for dir
steps.description:
Can you describe how it would solve the issue?

The use-cases here are (and please correct me if I'm wrong)

1. Using `atlantis plan -d 'prod/*'` to run all projects in `prod/*` space
2. Using `atlantis.yaml` to define wildcards for specific projects
    ```yaml
    version: 3
    projects:
    -
```yaml
locals {
workflow = "someworkflow"
}
```

steps.title: Upgrade atlantis to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade atlantis:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade atlantis atlantis/atlantis --namespace atlantis
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis
helm list -n atlantis
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get events -n atlantis --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow supplying wildcard for dir" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Can you describe how it would solve the issue?

The use-cases here are (and please correct me if I'm wrong)

1. Using `atlantis plan -d 'prod/*'` to run all projects in `prod/*` space
2. Using `atlantis.yaml` to define wildcards for specific projects
    ```yaml
    version: 3
    projects:
    - dir: dir1/**
      workflow: default
    - dir: dir2/**
      workflow: terragrunt
    ```

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with atlantis installed or the issue environment reproducible.
